summary: The Flysurfer Sonic V4 is a high-performance ram-air foil kite designed for racing and ultra-light wind foiling. Its very high aspect ratio delivers unmatched upwind performance and low-end power.
best_for: Foil racers and ultra-light wind specialists

sources.summary: The benchmark for foil racing kites. Riders praise the Sonic V4's incredible upwind angle and ability to generate power in virtually no wind.
Detailed review of the Sonic V4's racing performance, showcasing its superior upwind VMG and light wind capabilities compared to LEI alternatives.
Respected as the top open-cell foil kite for racing. Users note the relaunch difficulty is the main trade-off for its exceptional performance.

reviews.verdict: model right they haven't veered away too too far away from it in fact they've improved it in the past I always told myself I would not buy a Sonic because of my age and kiting style but I would definitely consider the Sonic 4. if you're a Sonic flyer the new Sonics I like it I really do so there you have it our review of the 10 meter fly Surfer Sonic 4. let us know in the comments below what you think and if you're excited to try this kite thank you for watching and we'll see you in the next one
reviews.full_transcript: it's always exciting when we've got a brand new kite to review and in this video we're going to be doing a deep dive on the 10 meter fly Surfer Sonic 4. [Music] [Applause] before we get started it's important to mention that kite reviews are tricky because the quality of wind can greatly influence how you feel about a kite for that reason when we test a kite we like to do it in a variety of conditions over multiple weeks and have our friends try the kite as well so that they can share their honest unbiased opinions many of the writers in this video are already fans of the Soul or the Sonic 3 so naturally there will be comparisons to both kites throughout this video as always a special thanks to silent sports for supporting our Channel and for making this review video happen the Sonic Forest fly Surfers high performance foil kite meant for Big Air hydrofoiling and Free Ride in our view while the Sonic 3 was a Pure Performance Beast that required expert level skills to handle the Sonic 4 is basically a supercharged sole it offers The Best of Both Worlds performance and user friendliness not surprisingly fly Surfer has designed the kite for those Riders who are looking for an upgrade from the soul or who already enjoy Sonic Heights and want more accessible performance so what's changed well according to Pfizer for pretty much everything as the Sonic 4 has a completely new design but this shouldn't scare you as it's still a Sonic most importantly the aspect ratio of the kite was slightly reduced to a range of 5.8 to 6.8 compared to the Sonic 3's aspect ratio range of 6.6 to 7.2 just like the Sonic 3 as the kite size decreases so does the aspect ratio another huge change are the sizes there's no longer a Sonic 9 11 or 13 meter and instead we have a Sonic 8 10 and 12 meter there's no more Sonic 21 but there is now a 6 meter on the Practical side according to fly Surfer the outline of the kite was redesigned to increase performance improve wingtip stability and improve launch behavior and turning we can't really comment about the performance of Sonic 4 versus Sonic 3 as comparing a 10 meter kite size versus an older 9 or 11 meter Sonic 3 wouldn't produce an objective comparison same goes for turning behavior from our tests the wing tips are as stable as the sole and will only fold if you oversteer other changes include the removal of mini ribs from the inside of the canopy but they remain on the Leading Edge just like the Sonic 3. there's also new adjustments on the Z main to change flight Behavior originally we had no idea what it was for so we did try it on various settings it basically changed the bar pressure from medium to high or low after trying all three we left it at the medium setting the fabric appears to be the same as the sole 2 and while we weren't crazy about the design at first it did eventually grow on us and we liked it in the end all right let's get into the rider reviews an important point to keep in mind is that none of the Riders including ourselves knew what had been changed or had any technical info about the kite at the time of filming so I had the opportunity to test out or try out the new Sonic Sonic 4 was is a wonderful kite it's a definite improvement over the two and three Sonics that I've tried I had the pleasure to try the 10 meter one here in in Hatteras so I've flown Seoul to sole three is there sulfury I have not been paid to do this interview I wish I was paid if I weren't looking up at this kite as far as the way it felt in the air indiscernible from the Sonic 3 to me identical bar feel to the the Sonic 3 so I have owned Sonic twos Sonic threes I fly the 11 90 of the time flew it all day yesterday and then came here and flew this so that's why I feel like the bar feel everything seems very very similar because I had a same line length too we put 17s on today my first impression flying the Sonic 4 was that it was actually quite stable it almost felt like flying a soul that tips didn't really kind of flap over the cuts flew really well which was surprising because normally when you fly a sonic you really have to be experienced you need to be able to handle the kite and fly it a certain way I must say that the modifications they've made has improved in how the Sonic flies seems a lot more stable I did notice that the Sonic 4 doesn't collapse like the Sonic 3. I find with the Sonic 3 every now and then you see the wing tips collapse depending on what I'm doing from my experience of flying Sonic 3 I found that it wasn't as forgiving in the past so with the new model Sonic 4 if you over fly the kite if you if you're doing a back roll and you land it a little bit short-sighted that Kai will forgive you if I make a mistake there's no Consequence the kite is not going to fall out of the sky lose its shape or anything of that sort so that way Sonic 4 is way closer to Seoul than to Sonic 3 and that to me is a big selling point it is a much much safer kite than the older Sonics the wingtips does not collapse like the older ones do the kite really feels like a soul on steroids it's got incredible boosting and jumping and power for a 10 meter kite for a big guy like me I would say in general less tip action but it's still there and it may just be the higher aspect kite and lighter or marginal wind so it that that is still there that behavior is still there but not as much as a Sonic 3. it's not a stable assault but it's definitely much more stable than Sonic 3 there's no wingtip collapse I mean you can collapse them if you over steer it but it will be a user error so the tips the tips problem is gone also too if you notice the inlets right on the new Sonic the inlet's a little closer to the tips right the end ones versus the Sonic 3 and that probably helps with keeping the tips get a little more stable getting air in there a lot quicker that way better behaved right on the edge of marginal wind like right at the edge of the window where the Sonic 3 will tend to Taco or tip fold um it still does that but it seems to be much more forgiving that way the the major difference I think is on a loop it has a way way bigger power Spike well it has a power Spike like a soul the Sonic doesn't you unless you have enough wind you don't get any extra oomph when you Loop the Sonic and the Sonic 3 this thing really pulled me out of the hole almost like a soul if I wasn't looking up I think it was a soul and you also feel it on a down Loop even off the wind it's got a a much bigger power Spike so maybe maybe better boosting I don't know I didn't get to try it much with much wind in order for me to go down when I had to continuously Loop the kite and it was a 14 mile down winter and uh I would say I did a couple hundred loops with this kite and you know what it Loops very nice very smooth as as the kite's going through the loop put generated the the pole that I needed when I needed it yeah no I I really do I'm definitely gonna get one of these right on this trip I flew both a 10 meter Sonic 4 and a 10 meter sole two now when I flew the Sonic 4 it was better win conditions it was a really smooth session I really enjoyed it the Sonic 4 was pretty stable the Soul 2 was also stable I don't know if I could really tell I felt a difference it did feel better when I flew the Sonic 4 but it was better win conditions so I don't know if it was the kite or if it was just a better wind yeah Sonic 4 was great it actually gives you enough time I think it's a lot more responsive than Seoul as when you've got some air time when you're boosting and gives you enough time to swing the kite back and forth the Sonic 4 launches as easily as the Sonic 3. the only thing that we noticed that was unlike the sole is that it may take a bit longer for the wingtips to fully inflate a solution is just to pull in the bar and back stall so the kite will open up from our test relaunching is noticeably improved as the new Sonic doesn't as easily bow tie compared to the previous iteration of the kite as you saw I dropped it in what five knots it was five knots Leading Edge down it seemed to want to come off the water backwards more than than a Sonic 3. did the usual foil crumple opened right up and it rolled over and came right off so relaunch seemed good the Sonic 4 is a great kite definitely an improvement over the Sonic 3 and previous Sonics I think that the Sonic 4 is a way more user friendly and beginner friendly than the Sonic 3 kite and it's very forgiving in terms of doing any back rolls or learning new new things with it it would be a lot more of a bigger bang for your buck for sure it really does feel a quarter of the way toward a soul from from Sonic like just just a little bit toward that behavior like the power the power Spike on a loop um the the more stable tips if the design point was to make it more like a soul it's uh I think I think that's achieved it's it's just a little less uh racy than than the Sonic 3. the modifications do that they've made to the new Sonic 4 does still let's call it adhere to the style of the Sonic right model right they haven't veered away too too far away from it in fact they've improved it in the past I always told myself I would not buy a Sonic because of my age and kiting style but I would definitely consider the Sonic 4. if you're a Sonic flyer the new Sonics I like it I really do so there you have it our review of the 10 meter fly Surfer Sonic 4. let us know in the comments below what you think and if you're excited to try this kite
